channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Raghav Chadha and six MPs join BJP, altering Rajya Sabha composition
item.description: In a major political development, Rajya Sabha MP Raghav Chadha, along with six other members, has left the Aam Aadmi Party (AAP) to join the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP). This has significantly change...

item.title: Netanyahu says early-stage prostate cancer was detected and fully treated after routine monitoring
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu announced that he was recently treated for a very early-stage malignant tumour in his prostate and has since fully recovered. The disclosure came as part of h...

item.title: Air Canada delivers delayed curling equipment to Wayne Middaugh after rerouting issue in Geneva
item.description: Canadian curler Wayne Middaugh raised concerns after arriving in Geneva, Switzerland, without his essential curling equipment ahead of the World Mixed Doubles and World Senior Curling Championships. P...

item.title: US prepares new Iran talks in Pakistan as ceasefire tensions and Strait of Hormuz crisis persist
item.description: The United States is preparing to resume negotiations with Iran, with senior envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner expected to travel to Pakistan for talks with Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghch...

item.title: DOJ says Zaldy Co’s location uncertain as talks with Czech authorities continue
item.description: Sinabi ni Justice Secretary Frederick Vida na walang kasiguraduhan kung nasa Czech Republic pa si dating Ako Bicol party-list Representative Zaldy Co, na nahaharap sa mga kasong korapsyon at malversat...

item.title: Vincent Escorts Stella Home in The Alibi Episode 28
item.description: In Episode 28 of ABS-CBN's popular series 'The Alibi,' viewers saw a touching moment between Vincent and Stella. Vincent made sure that Stella got home safely, personally accompanying her to her resid...

item.title: Roysambu Aspirant Reports Alleged Misuse of NG-CDF Funds and Illegal School Fees to EACC
item.description: James Munene Thuku, who is aspiring to become Roysambu MP, has lodged a petition with the Ethics and Anti-Corruption Commission (EACC) over alleged corruption in public education. Thuku, representing ...

item.title: Tourist Bus Catches Fire at EDSA Ortigas Flyover, Authorities Respond
item.description: A tourist bus caught fire late in the evening on April 24, 2026, along the EDSA Ortigas flyover. The incident occurred around 11:00 PM, and local authorities, including the Bureau of Fire Protection (...

item.title: US Embassy Gives Guidance for Kenyans Seeking Visas to Watch 2026 World Cup
item.description: The US, together with Canada and Mexico, will host the 2026 FIFA World Cup starting June. With new tougher visa regulations under President Trump, some citizens from flagged countries face partial or ...

item.title: Why $1 million in super may no longer guarantee a comfortable retirement
item.description: For years, Australians have viewed $1 million in superannuation as the benchmark for a comfortable retirement. However, this article argues that the figure no longer carries the same security it once ...

item.title: Updated Political Funding Tracker Launched to Mark PFA Anniversary
item.description: To mark the fifth anniversary of South Africa's groundbreaking Political Funding Act (PFA), a new version of the 'Whose Vote Counts' political funding tracking dashboard has been launched. The PFA, wh...

item.title: 16-Year-Old Sentenced for Involvement in Quadruple Homicide in Saskatchewan First Nation
item.description: A 16-year-old boy has been sentenced to three years after pleading guilty to four counts of manslaughter related to a quadruple homicide on the Carry the Kettle Nakoda Nation in February 2025. The vic...

item.title: Nvidia shares hit a fresh closing high as tech‑driven rally lifts U.S. indexes
item.description: Nvidia’s stock closed at a new record on Friday, marking its first record‑setting finish since October and underscoring the continued strength of the broader tech‑driven rally in U.S. equities. The Na...

item.title: Jammu Family Refutes Government Claims About Son’s Death in Russia-Ukraine Conflict
item.description: A family from Jammu has publicly refuted claims made by the central government regarding the death of their son in the ongoing Russia-Ukraine conflict. The government had earlier stated that their son...

item.title: ACT government demands urgent clarity from A-Leagues over Canberra United’s future
item.description: The ACT government has intensified pressure on the Australian Professional Leagues (APL) to resolve ongoing uncertainty surrounding Canberra United’s future in the A-League Women competition. Sport Mi...

item.title: Trump administration expands federal execution methods amid lethal injection drug shortages
item.description: The Trump administration plans to expand the federal government’s execution options to include firing squads, electrocution, and gas asphyxiation, in addition to lethal injection. The Justice Departme...

item.title: Survivors of Jeffrey Epstein speak out on abuse and activism
item.description: Saturday marks the first anniversary of Virginia Giuffre’s death, one of the first women to publicly disclose her experiences with Jeffrey Epstein and call for criminal accountability. Giuffre’s coura...

item.title: Hyderabad Police Introduces ‘Coffee with a Cop’ to Connect with Young People
item.description: In an effort to strengthen ties between law enforcement and the younger generation, the Cyberabad Police, in collaboration with the Society for Cyberabad Security Council (SCSC), has launched an innov...

item.title: Gatchalian proposes law restricting social media access for children under 16
item.description: Senator Sherwin Gatchalian has filed Senate Bill 2066, also known as the proposed Social Media Safety for Children Act, which aims to prohibit children below 16 years old from creating, accessing, or ...

item.title: Nova Scotia premier stands by 2025 woods ban despite court ruling citing Charter concerns
item.description: Nova Scotia Premier Tim Houston is defending his government’s controversial 2025 “woods ban,” despite a recent Supreme Court ruling that found the measure unreasonable. The ban, introduced on August 5...
